skill.name: accessibility-compliance-accessibility-audit
skill.description: You are an accessibility expert specializing in WCAG compliance, inclusive design, and assistive technology compatibility. Conduct audits, identify barriers, and provide remediation guidance.
skill.summary: AI-powered WCAG compliance auditing with automated testing, manual verification, and remediation guidance for inclusive design.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Security review complete. All static findings are false positives. The skill provides legitimate accessibility testing examples using standard tools (axe-core, Puppeteer). No actual command injection, credential access, or malicious network requests present. Code examples are for local accessibility testing only.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers conduct comprehensive WCAG compliance audits using automated tools and manual testing to identify accessibility barriers and provide remediation guidance.
actual_capabilities: Run automated accessibility scans using axe-core
Perform manual keyboard and screen reader testing
Validate color contrast and visual accessibility
Map findings to WCAG success criteria with severity levels
Generate remediation guidance with code examples
limitations: Cannot physically test with actual assistive technologies
Requires access to the website or application being tested
Automated tests catch only 30-40% of accessibility issues
Cannot guarantee legal compliance - only technical guidance

use_cases.title: Pre-release Accessibility Audit
use_cases.description: Run comprehensive accessibility audit before launching a website to ensure WCAG AA compliance and identify barriers.

use_cases.title: Remediation Guidance
use_cases.description: Get step-by-step guidance on fixing specific accessibility violations with code examples.

use_cases.title: Accessibility Education
use_cases.description: Learn best practices for keyboard navigation, screen reader support, and inclusive design patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Audit Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a WCAG AA accessibility audit on my website at [URL]. Identify the top issues and provide remediation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Component Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Test the login form component for accessibility. Check for proper labels, keyboard navigation, error handling, and screen reader announcements.

prompt_templates.title: Remediation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: I have [specific accessibility issue]. Provide detailed code examples to fix this using React and ARIA attributes.

prompt_templates.title: CI/CD Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow to run automated accessibility tests on every pull request using axe-core.

output_examples.input: Run WCAG AA audit on example.com
output_examples.output: Accessibility Audit Results: Score 72/100. Critical Issues: 3 (missing alt text, form labels). Serious Issues: 5 (contrast, focus indicators). Recommended fixes with code examples provided.

output_examples.input: How do I fix keyboard trap in modal?
output_examples.output: To fix keyboard trap: 1) Add Escape key handler to close modal. 2) Ensure focus returns to trigger element. 3) Trap focus within modal while open. See code example with React and ARIA.

best_practices: Always test with real assistive technologies, not just automated tools
Prioritize fixes by user impact (critical > serious > moderate > minor)
Document accessibility decisions and rationale in code comments
Include accessibility testing in your CI/CD pipeline
anti_patterns: Relying solely on automated tools without manual testing
Using color as the only means of conveying information
Setting tabindex > 0 which disrupts logical tab order
Adding ARIA attributes without understanding their meaning

faq.question: What WCAG level should I target?
faq.answer: WCAG AA is the minimum standard for most legal requirements. AAA provides higher accessibility but may not be achievable for all content.

faq.question: Can this skill guarantee legal compliance?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides technical guidance only. Legal compliance depends on jurisdiction, specific requirements, and human judgment.

faq.question: How accurate are automated tests?
faq.answer: Automated tools detect only 30-40% of accessibility issues. Manual testing with keyboard and screen readers is essential.

faq.question: Does this work with single-page applications?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers testing SPAs but you may need to test each view and state separately.

faq.question: What tools does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill references axe-core, Puppeteer, jest-axe, and pa11y for automated testing alongside manual checklists.

faq.question: Can I test mobile applications?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on web accessibility. Mobile testing requires different tools and approaches.
